Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check if DOWN cycles in the opposite direction
# Current position 2 symbol: cddd7d2c6a6ceea0 (cycle index 2)
# If DOWN goes backwards, it should go to index 1: b4edb3faf810133b

action(['DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
pink2 = [n['hash'] for n in nodes if n['id'] == 51][0]
print(f"After DOWN, position 2: {pink2}")

# Check if it went backwards in the cycle
cycle2 = ['6f89cc49ca8e1ebf', 'b4edb3faf810133b', 'cddd7d2c6a6ceea0', '2c0adb751e45fc49', '2c1efde5ada22c5a', 'f4469c244897540c', '5fcaa599a8d90976']
if pink2 in cycle2:
    print(f"  Cycle index: {cycle2.index(pink2)}")
    if cycle2.index(pink2) == 1:
        print("  DOWN goes backwards in cycle!")
    elif cycle2.index(pink2) == 3:
        print("  DOWN goes forwards in cycle (same as UP)!")
else:
    print("  NOT in cycle - DOWN might move cursor instead")

# Check cursor position
w = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'W']
for n in w:
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    print(f"Cursor: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check if DOWN cycles in the opposite direction
# Current position 2 symbol: cddd7d2c6a6ceea0 (cycle index 2)
# If DOWN goes backwards, it should go to index 1: b4edb3faf810133b

action(['DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
pink2 = [n['hash'] for n in nodes if n['id'] == 51][0]
print(f"After DOWN, position 2: {pink2}")

# Check if it went backwards in the cycle
cycle2 = ['6f89cc49ca8e1ebf', 'b4edb3faf810133b', 'cddd7d2c6a6ceea0', '2c0adb751e45fc49', '2c1efde5ada22c5a', 'f4469c244897540c', '5fcaa599a8d90976']
if pink2 in cycle2:
    print(f"  Cycle index: {cycle2.index(pink2)}")
    if cycle2.index(pink2) == 1:
        print("  DOWN goes backwards in cycle!")
    elif cycle2.index(pink2) == 3:
        print("  DOWN goes forwards in cycle (same as UP)!")
else:
    print("  NOT in cycle - DOWN might move cursor instead")

# Check cursor position
w = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'W']
for n in w:
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    print(f"Cursor: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
